risk_if_not_implemented: Without Event Log Backup on Separate Physical Systems / Components, security events may go undetected, allowing threats to persist unnoticed and increasing breach dwell time.

compensating_control_1.name: Cryptographic Hash
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to utilize hash algorithms to generate a hash value that can be used to validate the integrity of data and/or software.
compensating_control_1.justification: Cryptographic Hash (CRY-13) provides cryptographic protection that compensates for the absence of Event Log Backup on Separate Physical Systems / Components (MON-08.1) by ensuring data confidentiality and integrity through alternative technical means. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Event Log Retention
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to retain event logs for a time period consistent with records retention requirements to provide support for after-the-fact investigations of security incidents and to meet statutory, regulatory and contractual retention requirements.
compensating_control_2.justification: Event Log Retention (MON-10) provides detective monitoring capability that compensates for the absence of Event Log Backup on Separate Physical Systems / Components (MON-08.1) by identifying unauthorized, anomalous, or non-compliant activity that the primary control would have prevented. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
